name: plan_manifest_wave1_storage_bridge
description: Extend the app-manifest storage owner so control-plane manifests can carry status and lineage cleanly.

jobs.label: extend_manifest_storage_owner_for_control_plane
jobs.prompt:
## Goal
Update the storage owner for `app_manifests` so control-plane manifests can be persisted with explicit status and lineage instead of being second-class payload blobs.

## Read first
- `Code&DBs/Workflow/artifacts/workflow/_control_manifest_registry_contract.json`
- `Code&DBs/Workflow/storage/postgres/workflow_runtime_repository.py`
- `Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/016_app_builder_registries.sql`
- `Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/unit/test_canonical_manifests.py`

## Required code changes
Modify `Code&DBs/Workflow/storage/postgres/workflow_runtime_repository.py` so callers can set and preserve control-plane metadata when persisting manifest rows.

Concretely:
1. Extend `create_app_manifest(...)` to accept optional `parent_manifest_id` and `status` without breaking existing callers.
2. Extend `upsert_app_manifest(...)` to accept optional `parent_manifest_id` and `status` and persist them when provided.
3. Keep existing defaults for workspace/helm manifest callers.
4. Do NOT add new tables in this packet.
5. Do NOT route through helm normalization in the storage layer.

## Required tests
Create a dedicated test file:
- `Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/unit/test_workflow_runtime_repository_control_manifests.py`

Cover at least:
- create stores custom `status`
- create stores `parent_manifest_id`
- upsert preserves or updates status when passed
- existing default behavior still works when those fields are omitted

## Constraints
- Minimal API extension only. No new abstraction layer in this packet.
- The storage owner should remain generic: it stores manifest rows, not plan-specific logic.

Database: `postgresql://nate@127.0.0.1:5432/dag_workflow`
